text.title: Custom Error Type with HTTP Status
text.summary: AppError struct carrying Code, Message, Status, Details, and Err with Unwrap support.

rule.statement: AppError.Status must be private to the HTTP edge layer. Services should not know about HTTP status codes. The service returns an AppError; the handler reads its Status field.
rule.rationale: Putting HTTP status codes in domain services couples them to the transport layer. If the same service is later called via gRPC or a CLI, the HTTP status field is meaningless.

rule.statement: Pass a pointer to a pointer when using errors.As with struct types: errors.As(err, &appErr) where appErr is *AppError. Passing a value type silently fails to match.
rule.rationale: errors.As uses reflection to find the first error in the chain that can be assigned to the target. A value target (*AppError) must be a non-nil pointer; a value (AppError) always fails.

antipattern.description: var appErr AppError; errors.As(err, &appErr) — appErr is a value, not a pointer.
antipattern.reason: errors.As requires the target to be a non-nil pointer to either an error type or any type implementing error. Value types silently fail to match.
